summary.en: A manner clause tells how the main action happens. With 'as if / as though' it sets up a contrary-to-fact comparison, yet the main clause stays a confirmed fact; with 'as' it states a real, actual manner. Both can often be rephrased with 'the way'.
summary.zh: 方式从句说明主句动作“怎样”发生。用 as if / as though 时它做一个与事实相反的比较，但主句本身仍是确认的事实；用 as 时它陈述一种真实的方式。两者常可改写成 the way。

body.en: An adverbial clause of manner explains how the action of the main clause happens. The two most common kinds are introduced by 'as if / as though' (a hypothetical manner) and by 'as' (a real manner).
body.zh: 方式副词从句解释主句的动作“怎样”发生。最常见的有两种：由 as if / as though 引导（假想的方式），以及由 as 引导（真实的方式）。

heading.en: 'as if / as though' — a contrary-to-fact comparison
heading.zh: as if / as though — 与事实相反的比较

body.en: With 'as if / as though' the clause makes a hypothesis that is contrary to reality — it sounds like the subjunctive. The crucial difference: the main clause first states something as a confirmed fact, then the clause compares it to an unreal situation. In 'I feel good as if I just ate a carrot', 'I feel good' is true; 'as if I just ate a carrot' is the unreal comparison.
body.zh: 用 as if / as though 时，从句做一个与现实相反的假设——听上去很像虚拟语气。关键区别：主句先把某种情况交代为确认的事实，再用从句把它比作一个不真实的情况。在 “I feel good as if I just ate a carrot” 中，“I feel good” 是真的；“as if I just ate a carrot” 才是那个不真实的比较。

concepts.sourceRef: 这里的方式副词从句的主句直接就交代了某种情况，然后用从句交代一个与事实相反的假设

heading.zh: 方式从句 vs. 虚拟条件句

body.en: Compare the manner clause with a subjunctive conditional. 'If I had eaten a carrot, I would feel good' is contrary to past fact — it implies I did NOT eat a carrot, so 'I feel good' is NOT confirmed. In the manner version, 'I feel good' is already a fact; only the comparison is unreal.
body.zh: 把方式从句和虚拟条件句对比。“If I had eaten a carrot, I would feel good” 与过去事实相反——它暗示我刚才没吃胡萝卜，所以 “I feel good” 并不是确认的情况。而在方式从句版本里，“I feel good” 本身已是事实，只有那个比较是不真实的。

body.en: With 'as' there is no hypothesis at all — the clause states a real fact you can think of as a manner that is actually happening. 'Eat the carrot as I do' = eat it the same way I eat it; 'Leave the carrot as it is' = leave it the way it already is.
body.zh: 用 as 时完全没有假设——从句陈述一个真实的事实，可以理解为正在发生的某种方式。“Eat the carrot as I do” = 像我吃的那样吃；“Leave the carrot as it is” = 让它保持原本的样子。

concepts.sourceRef: 并不是假设，而是某种事实的阐述。你可以把它理解为正在发生的某种方式

body.en: Many manner clauses can be re-expressed with a 'the way' clause, which contains the very word 'way'. 'Eat the carrot as I do' becomes 'Eat the carrot the way I eat it.' One meaning, different clause types — some just sound more natural and concise.
body.zh: 很多方式从句可以用包含“way”一词的 the way 从句改写。“Eat the carrot as I do” 可以变成 “Eat the carrot the way I eat it.” 同一个意思，不同的从句类型——只是有些听上去更自然、更简洁。

examples.text: I feel good as if I just ate a carrot.

note.en: 'I feel good' is a confirmed fact; 'as if I just ate a carrot' is the contrary-to-fact comparison (I did not actually eat one).
note.zh: “I feel good”是确认的事实；“as if I just ate a carrot”是与事实相反的比较（我其实没吃）。

examples.text: If I had eaten a carrot, I would feel good.

note.en: A subjunctive conditional, contrary to past fact: it implies I did NOT eat a carrot, so 'I feel good' is not confirmed. Contrast this with the manner clause in e1.
note.zh: 虚拟条件句，与过去事实相反：暗示我没吃胡萝卜，所以“I feel good”并不确认。与 e1 的方式从句对比。

examples.text: You spent so much money as if you were a millionaire.

note.en: 'You spent so much money' is stated as fact; 'as if you were a millionaire' is the unreal comparison — but you are not one.
note.zh: “You spent so much money”被当作事实交代；“as if you were a millionaire”是不真实的比较——但你并不是百万富翁。

examples.text: Eat the carrot as I do.

note.en: 'As' here is a real manner, not a hypothesis: eat it the same way I actually eat it.
note.zh: 这里的“as”是真实的方式，不是假设：像我实际吃的那样吃。

examples.text: Leave the carrot as it is.

note.en: Leave it in the state it already is — a real, actual manner.
note.zh: 让它保持原本的样子——真实存在的方式。

examples.text: You spend money the way millionaires do.

note.en: A 'the way' clause rephrasing — the word 'way' itself carries the manner meaning.
note.zh: 用 the way 从句改写——“way”一词本身就承载方式的含义。

examples.text: Eat the carrot the way I eat it.

note.en: Same meaning as 'Eat the carrot as I do', expressed with a 'the way' clause — often more natural.
note.zh: 与“Eat the carrot as I do”同义，用 the way 从句表达——往往更自然。

prompt.en: In 'I feel good as if I just ate a carrot', what is the status of 'I feel good'?
prompt.zh: 在“I feel good as if I just ate a carrot”中，“I feel good”是什么状态？

explain.en: In a manner clause the main clause states a confirmed fact; only the 'as if' clause is the contrary-to-fact comparison.
explain.zh: 方式从句中主句交代的是确认的事实；只有 as if 从句才是与事实相反的比较。

prompt.en: Which introduces a REAL manner (no hypothesis)?
prompt.zh: 哪一句引导的是真实的方式（无假设）？

text.en: You spent so much money as if you were a millionaire.
text.zh: You spent so much money as if you were a millionaire.

text.en: Leave the carrot as it is.
text.zh: Leave the carrot as it is.

text.en: I feel good as if I just ate a carrot.
text.zh: I feel good as if I just ate a carrot.

explain.en: 'As it is' states a real, actual manner. The 'as if' clauses in the others are contrary-to-fact comparisons.
explain.zh: “as it is”陈述真实存在的方式。其余的 as if 从句都是与事实相反的比较。

prompt.en: Rewrite as a manner clause using 'as if': She walked in ____ ____ she owned the place.
prompt.zh: 用 as if 改写成方式从句：She walked in ____ ____ she owned the place.

explain.en: 'As if' (or the equivalent 'as though') sets up the contrary-to-fact manner comparison — she does not actually own the place.
explain.zh: “as if”（或同义的“as though”）引出与事实相反的方式比较——她其实并不拥有这地方。

prompt.en: Rephrase 'Eat the carrot as I do' with a 'the way' clause: Eat the carrot ____ ____ I eat it.
prompt.zh: 用 the way 从句改写“Eat the carrot as I do”：Eat the carrot ____ ____ I eat it.

explain.en: A 'the way' clause carries the manner meaning in the word 'way' and is often the more natural phrasing.
explain.zh: the way 从句把方式含义放在“way”一词里，往往更自然。

quizzes.sentence: You spent so much money as if you are a millionaire.
quizzes.fix: You spent so much money as if you were a millionaire.

explain.en: After 'as if' a contrary-to-fact comparison takes the past-subjunctive 'were', signalling you are not actually a millionaire.
explain.zh: “as if”后的与事实相反的比较用过去虚拟的“were”，表明你其实不是百万富翁。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: I feel good as if I just ate a carrot.

note.en: Main clause is a fact; the clause is the unreal comparison.
note.zh: 主句是事实；从句是不真实的比较。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: You spent so much money as if you were a millionaire.

note.en: Counterfactual manner — you are not actually rich.
note.zh: 与事实相反的方式——你其实并不富有。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Fine. Just eat the carrot as I do.

note.en: 'As' = a real, actual manner, no hypothesis.

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Or just leave the carrot as it is.

note.en: Leave it the way it already is — real manner again.
note.zh: 让它保持原样——同样是真实的方式。
